stories.headline: Typhoon Inday nears extreme Northern Luzon as enhanced monsoon soaks much of country
stories.summary: PAGASA said Typhoon Inday was about 640 kilometers east of Basco, Batanes, as of 3 a.m. Friday, July 10, with maximum sustained winds of 150 kph and gusts to 185 kph, moving northwestward at 20 kph. Signal No. 2 was raised over Batanes and parts of Cagayan and the Babuyan Islands, with Signal No. 1 over other northern areas. The bureau said the southwest monsoon, enhanced by the typhoon, would bring scattered to at times heavy rains over much of the country.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said Inday is forecast to be closest to extreme Northern Luzon between Friday night, July 10, and Saturday morning, July 11, before moving away from the Philippines.

stories.headline: Philippines marks 10 years of South China Sea ruling, calls it final and binding
stories.summary: The Department of Foreign Affairs marked the 10th anniversary of the 2016 arbitral award on the South China Sea, with Foreign Affairs Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro saying the ruling is final and legally binding and not subject to negotiation or compromise. The DFA held a commemorative conference in Pasay City on Friday, July 10, and with the Philippine Postal Corporation unveiled a stamp set marking the milestone. Officials said the government would keep anchoring its foreign policy on the ruling and UNCLOS.
stories.context: The July 12, 2016 ruling by an arbitral tribunal in The Hague rejected China's expansive claims in the South China Sea under UNCLOS. China has rejected the decision and continues to assert control over waters within the Philippines' exclusive economic zone.

stories.headline: Duterte impeachment trial ends first week; hearings to resume July 13
stories.summary: The Senate impeachment court completed the first week of Vice President Sara Duterte's trial, hearing the prosecution's case on an article alleging grave threats. A National Bureau of Investigation agent testified as the first witness, authenticating video of Duterte's November 2024 remarks, and the defense cross-examined him. The court granted a subpoena for Duterte's chief of staff, Zuleika Lopez. Duterte, who denies wrongdoing, did not appear inside the courtroom.
stories.context: The trial, which opened July 6, is the first of a sitting Philippine vice president. A conviction requires a two-thirds vote of the 24-member Senate and would remove Duterte from office and bar her from holding public office.
stories.watch_for: Hearings are scheduled to resume Monday, July 13, with Lopez expected to be called to testify.

stories.headline: Classes suspended across parts of Luzon, Visayas and Mindanao over typhoon, monsoon and ashfall
stories.summary: Local governments called off classes for Friday, July 10, in areas across Luzon, the Visayas and Mindanao, citing Typhoon Inday, the southwest monsoon, and ashfall from Kanlaon Volcano. Suspensions covered several Metro Manila cities, provinces such as Aurora, and towns in Cebu, Antique and Negros Occidental and Oriental, among others.

stories.headline: Philippine inflation eases to 6.4 percent in June on cheaper fuel
stories.summary: The Philippine Statistics Authority said headline inflation slowed to 6.4 percent in June from 6.8 percent in May, driven mainly by softer transport costs as global oil prices fell. Food inflation also eased, to 5.2 percent. The PSA said inflation averaged 4.8 percent in the first half of 2026, still above the government's 2 to 4 percent target, while core inflation rose to 4.4 percent.

stories.headline: Kanlaon Volcano stays on Alert Level 2 after explosive eruption dusts Negros and Cebu
stories.summary: Phivolcs said Kanlaon Volcano in Negros produced a moderately explosive eruption at its summit crater on Thursday, July 9, sending up an ash plume that, carried by winds, dusted parts of Negros and several towns in Cebu. The volcano stayed under Alert Level 2, and Phivolcs kept the four-kilometer permanent danger zone off-limits and barred flights near the summit.
stories.context: Alert Level 2 indicates increasing unrest and the possibility of further sudden explosions. Kanlaon has erupted several times over the past year.

stories.headline: DFA launches online visa system at Philippine posts in Europe
stories.summary: The Department of Foreign Affairs said it launched its e-Visa system on July 6 at selected Philippine foreign service posts in Europe, including the embassy in Copenhagen, which covers Denmark, the Faroe Islands and Greenland. The DFA said the online platform lets eligible foreign nationals apply for a temporary visitor's visa before traveling, and that visa-free entry of up to 30 days for Danish passport holders remains in place.

stories.headline: Philippine jobless rate edges up to 4.8 percent in May as farm work declines
stories.summary: The Philippine Statistics Authority said the unemployment rate rose to 4.8 percent in May from 4.7 percent in April, leaving about 2.5 million Filipinos jobless, with losses in agriculture weighing on the labor market. The number of employed rose to 49.63 million as more people joined the labor force. The PSA said underemployment improved, falling to 12.2 percent from 15.2 percent in April.

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz shipping slows sharply as US and Iran resume fighting
stories.summary: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz fell steeply after renewed US-Iran fighting, with monitors reporting few large vessels using a US-coordinated route in recent days compared with dozens earlier in the week. Iran said it struck US military sites in several Gulf states in response to American strikes on Iranian targets. Brent crude held near 76 dollars a barrel, though analysts warned of further price pressure if the disruption persists.

stories.headline: At least 28 killed in shoe factory fire in southeastern China
stories.summary: At least 28 people were killed in a fire at a shoe factory in Jinjiang, in China's Fujian province, on Thursday, July 9, state media said. Authorities said 239 people were in the building when the blaze broke out around midday and 213 were evacuated. State media said flammable materials stored inside likely helped the fire spread quickly, and that company officials had been taken into custody.

stories.headline: Ukraine strikes Russian oil sites far from front as Russia launches drone barrage
stories.summary: Ukraine said it hit several Russian oil facilities, including depots in the Tver and Stavropol regions and a pumping station in Ufa far from the front line, and set two tankers ablaze in the Sea of Azov. President Volodymyr Zelenskyy described the campaign as long-range sanctions. Ukrainian officials said Russia had fired 94 drones and two missiles overnight, most of the drones intercepted, with damage reported at 13 locations.

stories.headline: France beats Morocco 2-0 to reach World Cup semifinals
stories.summary: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in a 2026 World Cup quarterfinal, with Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembele scoring in the second half to send the team to the semifinals.
